title: Credit-Scoring Decision Tree: Design the Split
strategies: no-split majority leaf: predict the training-set majority class from a single leaf; reproduces the evaluator's majority-class baseline (~0.1) and models none of the risk regions

shallow Gini tree: greedy top-down splits minimising sample-weighted Gini impurity, capped at depth 3 with no leaf-size guard; carves the biggest risk region but underfits the population's several disjoint regions

deep regularised Gini tree: same impurity criterion grown to depth 8 with a min-samples-per-leaf floor; isolates each risk region while the leaf-size floor avoids memorising noise, maximising held-out accuracy without hitting 1.0
invalid: emit a structurally broken tree (out-of-range child index / cycle / non-finite threshold) -> rejected, scores 0
